summary: The filing says how many people the plan covers — not who they are or where they live. This page models that. We start from the company's store locations, then use the Census Bureau's journey-to-work data — real, anonymized commuting flows between home and work — to estimate where the people who work at each location live.

On that geography we place a synthetic workforce: thousands of computer-generated individuals drawn from Census-based population data, matched to this workforce's age and income profile, each at a realistic household location. Every dot on the map is synthetic — no real person's data appears — but in aggregate the cohort looks and behaves like the real workforce. When a client shares an actual census, the real roster replaces this model.

moves.headline: Locate the worksites
moves.detail: Store locations from OpenStreetMap, geocoded to 2020 Census blocks (the geography the commute data uses).

moves.headline: Follow the commute data home
moves.detail: members(tract) = participants × [retail-sector commute flows from that tract into the worksite blocks ÷ all such flows]. Flows are LEHD LODES8 origin–destination (vintage 2023, main + aux), retail super-sector (SI02); the workforce age and earnings profiles are the SA/SE marginals over the same flows.

moves.headline: Populate with synthetic people
moves.detail: Tracts sampled with probability proportional to modeled members; individuals drawn from the Census-based synthetic population and reweighted so the cohort matches the workforce's age shape (LODES) and earnings distribution (individual earnings lifted to household income by ×1.6). Each twin carries age, sex, race/ethnicity, and household income at a realistic household coordinate.

moves.headline: Add modeled health & engagement
moves.detail: Each twin also carries a modeled clinical and engagement profile — see the Clinical cost drivers methodology for how those are attached.

limitations: A modeled estimate of where members live — never actual member addresses.
Commute flows are block-level and cover every employer in a block; blocks dominated by a single big-box store are a good proxy, mixed-use blocks are noisier.
Age and earnings inputs are coarse 3-band Census marginals; no finer precision is claimed.
deviation_from_methodology: METHODOLOGY.md states OD has no industry/age/earnings segments; the LODES8 OD files DO (SA/SE/SI columns, verified). This pipeline uses those directly instead of combining WAC/RAC under assumption.
